english: rig-gis (rig gis) by an extraordinary manifestation of power or strength Dzl.; klui mtu yin (klu'i mthu yin) that is an effect of the Lus (lus) Stg.; mtus (mthus) by virtue of, frq.; mtu-stóbs (mthu stobs) = mtu (mthu). — 2. magic, witchcraft, mtu _tóń-ba (mthu 'thong ba) Mil., mtu _débs-pa (mthu 'debs pa), *táb-cè* (*thab ce*) W., to practise witchcraft, to injure a person by magic spells, to bewitch Mil. and col. frq.; mtu ser brtad ysúm-po rdzógs-par bslabs (mthu ser brtad gsum po rdzogs par bslabs) conjuring, raising tempests, exorcising ghosts, all these things I have learned thoroughly Mil.; mtu-bo-čé (mthu bo che) high-potent, high and mighty Tar.

mtun-pa (mthun pa), also _tun-pa ('thun pa), to agree, to harmonize; agreement, harmony; agreeing etc., 1. in a general sense, c. c. dań (dang), .. yin-par don mtun-no (yin par don mthun no) they agree in the opinion of her being... Glr.; mtun-par byéd-pa (mthun par byed pa) to make agree, to bring to an agreement, to make consistent, mtun-par _gyúr-ba (mthun par 'gyur ba) to be made agreeing or consistent Glr.; dgóńs-pa yčig-tu mtun-pa (dgongs pa gcig tu mthun pa) unanimous; lhai lugs dań mtun (lha'i lugs dang mthun) god-like (in deeds) Glr.; rigs mtun-pa (rigs mthun pa) of equal birth; lo mtun-pa (lo mthun pa) of the same age, contemporary; blo mtun-te (blo mthun te) being of the same mind, similarly disposed, čos byá-bar (chos bya bar) with respect to religion Glr.; ka mtun-par (kha mthun par) with one mouth, gros mtun-par (gros mthun par) with one accord, unanimously, as one man; grabs mtun-pa (grabs mthun pa) to live in harmony; — to be adequate, corresponding to, e.g. yid (dań) mtun-par (yid (dang) mthun par), resp. tugs dań mtun-par (thugs dang mthun par), to one's wish, as one could desire = yid bźin-du (yid bzhin du); nad dań mtun-par (nad dang mthun par) corresponding to the disease, fit or proper for the disease. — 2. in a special sense 1. viz. yid dań (yid dang), to be wished for, desirable, particularly in mtun-rkyén (mthun rkyen), v. rkyén (rkyen); also: to wish, to like, to delight in, kyéd-rnams-kyis mtun-paí rdzas (khyed rnams kyis mthun pa'i rdzas) things wished for by you, desirable to you Mil.; 2. with or without yčig-la yčig (gcig la gcig): mtun-nas (mthun nas) whenever they (the two nations) lived in peace with each other (opp. to _kón-nas ('khon nas)) Glr.; mtun-par rtam byéd-pa (mthun par gtam byed pa) to converse amicably Glr., to enter into negotiations of peace Glr.; mtun-par byéd-pa (mthun par byed pa) 1. v. above, 2. to caress, to fondle, to dandle Glr.; šiń-tu mtun-par yod (shin tu mthun par yod) they are on the best terms with each other, are making love to each other Glr.; mtun-po bsdad _dug (mthun po bsdad 'dug) col. id.; mtun-po byéd-pa (mthun po byed pa) to be kind, affable, condescending Mil. (opp. to being proud, cold, reserved); rgya bod yñis mtun _oń (rgya bod gnyis mthun 'ong) there will be a good understanding between China and Tibet Glr.; mi mtun-paí pyogs tams-čád-las rgyál-ba (mi mthun pa'i phyogs thams cad las rgyal ba) to gain the victory over all the hostile parties; mtun-_gyur-gyi yi-ge (mthun 'gyur gyi yi ge) C. letter of recommendation; mtun-čan (mthun can) W. gentle, peaceful.

mtur (mthur), also mtúr-mgo (mthur mgo), v. tur-mgo (thur mgo), halter, rta-mtur (rta mthur) Lex. id.; mtur-tág (mthur thag) rein, reins Sch.; mtur-mtá (mthur mtha') the end of the reins, e.g. to place them into the hands of another.

col. mté-bóń (mthe bong), mte-čén (mthe chen), mteb-mo (mtheb mo) (v. also te bo (the bo)), thumb, rkáń-paí mté-bo (rkang pa'i mthe bo) the big toe; mteb-čuń (mtheb chung) the little finger, the little toe Glr.
1. a little hammer; 2. mteu-čuń (mthe'u chung) the little toe.

1. a span, from the end of the thumb to the end of the middle finger when extended; mto _jál-ba (mtho 'jal ba) or yźál-ba (gzhal ba), W. *táb-če* (*thab che*), to span, to measure by the hand with the fingers extended; mto gáń (mtho gang), mto ré tsam (mtho re tsam) a span (in length), mto do (mtho do) two spans. — 2. v. mtó-ba (mtho ba).
a little triangular receptacle into which the likeness of an enemy is placed, to whom one wishes to do harm by witchcraft W.

1. to be high; highness, height; high, lofty, elevated, B. (cf. mtóń-po (mthong po)), frq. fig; rigs če-źiń mtó-ba-ste (rigs che zhing mtho ba ste) being of high and noble birth Dzl.; dé-las mtó-ba (de las mtho ba) more elevated than that, surpassing, surmounting that; c. accus. or instrum., high
